acceptance_targets: [ch16-craft-01] PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9-29 contains at least one live action exchange in which student resistance or Carrow collapse changes the corridor situation while Gavin is present, with specific bodily or sensory consequences on the page; FAIL if those developments are still conveyed chiefly through retrospective summary.

[ch16-craft-02] PASS if a reader can place revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:53-55 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:171-183 unambiguously before the Night of May 1 to dawn of May 2, 1998 battle window without contradicting the chapter's own date language; FAIL if the chapter still signals both an early-May battle night and a late-April continuity slot at once.

[ch16-award-01] PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:31-47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:121-141, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:163-183 each introduce a distinct new condition or consequence, and only one of those spans carries the full explanation of why seventeen simultaneous variables exceed Gavin's cognition; FAIL if two or more spans still perform the same read-fail-explain loop.

[ch16-prose-01] PASS if no more than two of the cited spans still rely on a negation-then-correction construction and the revised sentences state the perception directly; FAIL if three or more of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:75, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:179 still use the same formula.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9-29 contains at least one live action exchange in which student resistance or Carrow collapse changes the corridor situation while Gavin is present, with specific bodily or sensory consequences on the page; FAIL if those developments are still conveyed chiefly through retrospective summary.
findings.problem: The opening handles the chapter's most volatile material as retrospective digest rather than live dramatic pressure. Open resistance, disappearances into the Room, corridor blood, and the Harry rumor all arrive as summary paragraphs before Gavin enters a scene with consequences. Under the constitution's sanitization rule, this is summary replacing scene at the point where the novel is supposed to make the school's collapse physically immediate.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace part of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9-19 with a real-time corridor beat anchored around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:21-29 or inserted just before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:31. Put Gavin in the middle of one concrete act of DA logistics, Carrow authority failure, or rumor-driven movement so the chapter earns its chaos through present action instead of recap while preserving his detached priorities.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can place revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:53-55 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:171-183 unambiguously before the Night of May 1 to dawn of May 2, 1998 battle window without contradicting the chapter's own date language; FAIL if the chapter still signals both an early-May battle night and a late-April continuity slot at once.
findings.problem: The chapter's date cues drift against canon continuity. The text explicitly places the afternoon in 'early May' and then frames the castle as battle-night 'tonight,' but the continuity sheet places chapter_16's resistance escalation in late April 1998 and reserves the Night of May 1 to dawn of May 2, 1998 for chapter_17. As written, chapter_16 muddies the canonical boundary between pre-battle deterioration and the battle window.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:53-55 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:171-183, either remove or reframe the early-May/battle-night cues so the chapter stays clearly in the late-April slot established by context/cycle_02/continuity_sheet.json, or add an explicit temporal transition that preserves chapter_17 as the May 1 battle night.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:31-47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:121-141, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:163-183 each introduce a distinct new condition or consequence, and only one of those spans carries the full explanation of why seventeen simultaneous variables exceed Gavin's cognition; FAIL if two or more spans still perform the same read-fail-explain loop.
findings.problem: The chapter's architecture loops the same work beat too many times: Gavin sits with the manuscript, explains why Builder maintenance exceeds him, and keeps reading. The wrist scene is the true change event, but the surrounding first and third movements echo each other so closely that the chapter reads as connective tissue rather than a distinct escalation on the eve of battle.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or merge one of the explanatory work loops in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33-47 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:121-141, then use the recovered space to sharpen the irreversible turn in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:163-183 or to deepen the live human pressure before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:31. The chapter should move through three different states, not restate one impossibility three times.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two of the cited spans still rely on a negation-then-correction construction and the revised sentences state the perception directly; FAIL if three or more of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:75, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:179 still use the same formula.
findings.problem: A repeated negation-then-correction sentence shape becomes a prose tic: 'Not X, not Y, but Z,' 'Not healed - corrected,' 'not wrongness, exactly.' Used this often in one chapter, the construction stops feeling like Gavin thinking his way toward precision and starts feeling like an authorial fallback rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast several of the cited sentences in direct affirmative language, especially snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:139-145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:179. Keep the negation/correction move only where the conceptual correction is itself the dramatic point, and vary the surrounding syntax everywhere else.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
